narrative: Dear [Student's Name], I know this rejection letter stings—dreaming of law school only to have your application turned away feels like a door slamming shut just as you're reaching for the handle. Our prediction model looked at your profile and estimated a 54% chance you'd struggle to pass the bar exam, which is why we had to make this tough call. What stood out most was your Year 3 decile of 1.0, the lowest ranking in your school, signaling to the model that your performance dipped significantly by the end of undergrad, pulling hardest toward this concerning outlook. Right behind that was your Year 1 decile, also a 1.0, showing that early struggles set a pattern the model couldn't ignore. Your LSAT score of 19.0 reinforced this, as it fell well below what typically predicts bar success, adding another strong push toward failure. Coming from a low family income bracket made it even tougher in the model's eyes, hinting at potential barriers like fewer resources for prep or support. Your Asian background played a role too, though smaller, tilting things further in that direction based on patterns it's seen before. Even your undergraduate GPA of 3.0, while solid on paper, nudged the prediction downward when stacked against these others. Your choice to go full-time weighed in negatively as well, suggesting the intense workload might amplify risks. The one bright spot was your gender—being female actually pulled slightly toward success in the model's view. But overall, this combination painted a picture of someone whose academic trajectory and circumstances have historically led to bar exam hurdles, even with your clear passion for law. This isn't the end of your story; many have turned similar profiles around with targeted prep, and we truly hope you keep pursuing your goals. Take care.
